content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN binary strings of length NNN each. You need to find a binary string of length NNN which is different from all of the given strings.

Note:


A binary string is defined as a string consisting only of `'0'` and `'1'`.
A string is considered different from another string when they have different lengths, or when they differ in at least one position.

Input Format


The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.




The first line of each test case contains NNN  - the number of strings and length of strings.




Each of the next NNN lines contains a binary string of length NNN.





Output Format
For each test case, print on one line a binary string of length NNN, which is different from all of the given strings. If there are multiple possible answers, print any.
constraints: Each string consists only of characters '0' and '1' and has length exactly N.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N – the number of binary strings and also their length. Then follow N lines, each containing a binary string of length N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output on a separate line a binary string of length N that is different from every given string. Any valid string is accepted.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: diagonal bits are 1,1,0 → flipped to 0,0,1 giving \"001\". Test case 2: diagonal bits are 1,0,0,0 → flipped to 0,1,1,1 giving \"0111\".

solution.approach: Use the classic diagonal argument. For each position i (0‑based) look at the i‑th character of the i‑th input string. If it is '0' put '1' in the answer at position i, otherwise put '0'. The constructed string differs from the i‑th string at position i, therefore it differs from every given string. This runs in O(N) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – we read each character once and build the answer in linear time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) – to store the N input strings and the answer.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        strings = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = ['1' if strings[i][i] == '0' else '0' for i in range(N)]
        out_lines.append(''.join(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
